name: tool-use
summary: Agent uses external tools (APIs, shells, MCP servers) to take actions beyond pure reasoning. The foundational pattern — everything else builds on it.
when_to_use: Any task requiring real-world side effects: reading files, running tests, calling APIs, writing code. Claude Code always uses tool-use.

layers.CLAUDE.md: Document tool preferences: 'Prefer Read over Bash(cat); Glob over Bash(find); Edit over sed.'
layers.skill: skills/hooks for lifecycle gates around tool calls.
layers.hook: PreToolUse / PostToolUse for governance, logging, transformation.
layers.agent: Agents inherit tool restrictions via allowed-tools frontmatter.

cost: Baseline. Tool calls themselves are cheap; the risk is unbounded tool loops.
anti_patterns: Tools called without error handling — crashes the chain.
Unbounded tool loops — no stopping criterion.
Using raw Bash when a dedicated tool exists (Read, Edit, etc.) — slower + less reviewable.
